claims.evidence: FAIL: Line 55 shows 'ggplot(data = <DONNÉES>, mapping = aes(<CORRESPONDANCES>)) + <FONCTION_GEOM>()' — the template placeholder text inside the plain (non-R) code block was incorrectly translated from English to French. Source has <DATA>, <MAPPINGS>, <GEOM_FUNCTION>(). This is a markup preservation failure not caught by current assertions.

suggestions.reason: Critical gap: no assertion covers plain code blocks (``` without {r ...}). This run translated <DATA>→<DONNÉES>, <MAPPINGS>→<CORRESPONDANCES>, <GEOM_FUNCTION>()→<FONCTION_GEOM>() inside the plain code block template, breaking the R code. The with-skill run correctly preserved this. A 'plain-code-blocks-preserved' assertion would turn this into a measurable FAIL rather than an uncaught bug.

eval_feedback.overall: All defined assertions pass, but there is an uncaught markup preservation failure in the plain code block. The current assertions do not discriminate the meaningful quality difference between with-skill and without-skill for this eval.
